title: RocketRaid
description: RocketRaid transforms Minecraft's crossbows into powerful RPG launchers.
body:
![Preview of RocketRaid](https://ezexzo.com/assets/projects/rocketraid-preview.gif)

RocketRaid is a simple and powerful plugin that turns fireworks into explosive RPGs when fired from a crossbow. Designed for PvP and raid-heavy servers, RocketRaid adds a fun and strategic twist by allowing players to craft or receive special “Rocket” fireworks that explode like TNT on impact.

# Compatible with Spigot 1.21 and up
v1.0.0: Initial release with core functionality

# How it Works
Players can craft or receive a firework named “Rocket.” When this Rocket is fired from a crossbow, it creates a TNT-style explosion on impact. Only fireworks with the exact name "Rocket" will trigger the effect, keeping your gameplay controlled and balanced.

# Crafting
![Rocket Crafting Recipe](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b8fc13a0d3162b0d87552d40338518713804fb08.png)

Players can craft a Rocket using a shapeless recipe:
- 1x Gunpowder
- 1x Paper
- 1x Fire Charge

These ingredients can be placed in any arrangement in the crafting grid. The result will be a custom firework rocket named "Rocket" that can be loaded into a crossbow and fired for an explosive effect.



# Features
- Explosive firework rockets when fired from crossbows
- Shapeless crafting recipe using 1 Gunpowder, 1 Paper, and 1 Fire Charge
- Command to give Rockets directly to players
- Only named Rockets explode to prevent misuse
- Lightweight and survival-friendly design

# Installation / Configuration / Usage
Drop the RocketRaid jar file into your server’s plugins folder. Restart the server or use a plugin manager to load it. Players can craft Rockets or use the /rocketraid give command to receive them.

# Commands
/rocketraid – Displays plugin name, version, and author
/rocketraid give – Gives a Rocket firework to the player
/rocketraid give [player] – Gives a Rocket firework to the specified player

# Permissions
rocketraid.give – Allows use of the give command (default: OP)

# Dependencies
None required. Just install and boom.
